text: In recent years, artificial intelligence has become an important part of our daily lives. However, the rise of AI also brings serious ethical concerns. One major issue is bias. If the data used to train AI systems contains unfair patterns, the AI can make biased decisions, for example in job recruitment or loan approvals. This raises questions about fairness. Privacy is another key challenge, as AI often collects large amounts of personal information without clear consent. To address these problems, transparency is essential. People should understand how AI systems reach their conclusions. Ultimately, responsible AI development requires careful planning, diverse teams, and ongoing monitoring to ensure that technology benefits everyone fairly.
